More Notes: Total premature deaths attributable to wildland fire PM2.5 over the 11-year period (2008-2018), so using the middle year as representative
Economic impact associated with premature deaths over the 11-year period (2008-2018), so using the middle year as representative
More than 3600 deaths were attributed to California wildfires in 2018, with total damages reported at more than $148 billion.
The reported mortality is the highest observed for the base case scenario with no data capping, reflecting the year with the highest number of wildfire acres burned during the analysis period.
This is the total mortality for the entire 2008-2018 period under the modified cap scenario, showing the impact when extreme values are excluded.
This estimate is about 35% lower than the projection using the wildfire-specific dose-response value, demonstrating variability in mortality when using a general PM2.5 coefficient.
The result represents the upper end of the range for the period from 2008 to 2018; 2018 is highlighted as a high-impact year.
The economic valuation reflects associated costs of premature deaths.
Attributable to fire-only PM2.5 exposures; average over 2008-2018, using midpoint as representative year.
Associated with premature deaths from fire-only PM2.5 exposures; average over 2008-2018, using midpoint as representative year.
